1.text: Braun & Clarke 6-Phase Thematic Analysis | v42 | 20 March 2026

text_boxes.text:
• Six PMF themes from Braun & Clarke analysis (v42) on 3 GCC customer interviews (Accenture, Baker Hughes, Shell)
• This deck follows the standard structure: methodology, strategic context, full PESTEL, competitive and Win/Loss, primary research, triangulation, then deep-dive themes, RICE, and E2E handoff
• Strongest signals: nationalization compliance, candidate grid friction, AI matching, WhatsApp-first comms, interview scheduling complexity, native reporting gaps (PowerBI / Excel exits)
• E2E handoff lists 8 actionable recommendations for PM selection

speaker_notes:
• Executive overview; next sections mirror prior GCC deck structure (PESTEL, competitive, Win/Loss, interviews).
• Deep theme slides reflect v42 coding pass.

References:
• research/GCC/thematic-analysis/2026-03-20-GCC-PMF-Analysis-v42.md

text_boxes.text: Research Challenge
Objectives and methodology

• How can Workday Recruiting better support hiring in the GCC region?
• Understand customer pain points, compliance requirements, and local preferences
• Identify product gaps and roadmap opportunities
• Triangulate customer interviews with Win/Loss data and PESTEL context

speaker_notes:
• Braun & Clarke 6-phase thematic analysis.
• 3 customer interviews, 2 GCC Win/Loss gaps.

References:
• Braun & Clarke (2006) Thematic Analysis

text_boxes.text:
Phase 0: Geographic filtering (GCC)
Phase 1: Familiarisation with data
Phase 2: Generating initial codes
Phase 3: Generating themes
Phase 4: Reviewing themes (triangulation)
Phase 5: Defining and naming themes
Phase 6: Producing report + slide deck

speaker_notes:
• Standard Braun & Clarke methodology.

References:
• Braun & Clarke (2006)

text_boxes.text: Context Review
Strategic context and market momentum

• GCC HR Tech: US$2.6B (2023) → US$5.5B (2032), CAGR 9.05%
• 70% of GCC companies plan to invest in HR tech
• Vision 2030 (Saudi) and economic diversification drive digital transformation
• Nationalization mandates (Nitaqat, Emiratisation) accelerate ATS adoption

speaker_notes:
• Astute Analytica market data.

References:
• https://www.astuteanalytica.com/industry-report/gcc-hr-tech-market

text_boxes.text: PESTEL Analysis
Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, Legal

• Saudi Nitaqat 2026–2028: 340,000+ jobs localised; sector quotas (tourism 40%, engineering 30%, marketing 60%)
• UAE Emiratisation: 8% (2025), 10% (2026); AED 108,000 per missing Emirati
• Kuwait Kuwaitisation, Oman Omanisation: Similar mandates
• Non-compliance: visa freezes, licence suspension, government contract exclusion

Product implication: OOB nationalization tracking as native dimensions with application capture and compliance dashboards.

speaker_notes:
• Mondaq, Envoy Global sources.

References:
• https://www.mondaq.com/saudiarabia/contracts-and-commercial-law/1754286

text_boxes.text:
• GCC HR Tech: US$2.6B → US$5.5B by 2032 (9.05% CAGR)
• 70% of GCC companies investing in HR tech
• Recruitment/ATS key segment
• Enterprise software consolidation in Saudi/UAE

Product implication: GCC is high-growth market; nationalization and recruiting capabilities are differentiators.

speaker_notes:
• Astute Analytica.

References:
• https://www.astuteanalytica.com/industry-report/gcc-hr-tech-market

text_boxes.text:
• WhatsApp: 98% open rate vs 20% email; 30M+ users in Saudi; dominant for recruitment
• GCC smartphone penetration: Saudi 92%, UAE 97% (highest in MENA)
• P2: 40%+ career site traffic from mobile in Middle East
• Arabic/English bilingual: mix for professional vs blue-collar roles

Product implication: WhatsApp integration; mobile-optimised apply flow; Arabic RTL support.

speaker_notes:
• Statista, Wuseller.

References:
• https://www.statista.com/statistics/1190185/mena-smartphone-penetration-rate-forecast-by-region/

text_boxes.text:
• Qiwa, GOSI, Mudad: Saudi government portals for work permits, Nitaqat dashboard
• 70% GCC companies investing in HR tech; HiredScore, Paradox integrations
• AI-powered recruitment platforms and workforce analytics

Product implication: Government portal integrations; AI-assisted screening and scheduling.

speaker_notes:
• Qiwa platform, Astute Analytica.

References:
• https://safwahr.com/qiwa-platform-complete-employer-guide-saudi-arabia-2026/

text_boxes.text:
• DATA GAP: No material recruiting-specific environmental signals
• UAE Net Zero 2050, Saudi 2060 are macro
• ESG workforce reporting may emerge

speaker_notes:
• Environmental factor has limited recruiting relevance.

References:
• N/A

text_boxes.text:
• Saudi PDPL 2023: Effective Sept 2023; SDAIA oversight; consent, breach 72h, DPO, DPIA
• KSA Interview Regulations (May 2025): 3-day notice; panel ≥2 Saudi nationals (1 HR specialist); non-Saudi ≤50%; 30-day outcome notification
• UAE PDPA 2022: Data protection requirements

Product implication: Interview scheduling must enforce 3-day notice, panel composition validation; consent capture; PDPL-compliant candidate data handling.

speaker_notes:
• Mondaq, Lexology, DLA Piper.

References:
• https://www.mondaq.com/saudiarabia/privacy-protection/1704024
• https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=c7de6aaf-c00f-40ea-bb2e-c2c11da83e07

text_boxes.text: Competitive Landscape
Regional specialists vs global platforms

Global platforms: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM/Taleo, ADP
Regional specialists: ZenHR (ZenATS), Talentera (Bayt.com), Darwinbox
Differentiators: ZenHR/Talentera offer native nationalisation tracking, Arabic localisation, government portal awareness
Workday wins: Enterprise HCM integration
Workday loses: GCC-specific compliance OOB

speaker_notes:
• ZenHR built for MENA; Talentera Bayt.com.

References:
• https://blog.zenhr.com/en/the-complete-hr-stack-for-multi-entity-gcc-groups-2026-executive-guide

text_boxes.text: Win/Loss Overview
GCC-explicit gaps

rows: GCC populations cannot use WD integrations with Outlook for interview scheduling or MS Teams HiredScore
CareerPlug integration; prospect may post jobs in competitor

speaker_notes:
• 2 GCC-explicit gaps from filtered dataset.

References:
• research/raw-data/filtered_gcc_opps.csv

text_boxes.text: Primary Research
Interview participants

P1 – Cyber Security & Campus Hiring Lead, Accenture (Egypt, GCC, Africa)
P2 – Performance & Innovation Manager, Baker Hughes (Global TA)
P3 – Product Owner, Talent & Resourcing, Shell (Global, franchise countries including GCC)

speaker_notes:
• Anonymised as P1, P2, P3 per research protocol.

References:
• research/GCC/customer-transcripts/

text_boxes.text:
• Nationalization: 20% Emiratisation, 60% Saudization, 50% Kuwaitisation; need to track throughout Workday
• Interview scheduling: 'If we can schedule interviews and send notifications... a lot better'; KSA 3-day notice, 50% Saudi panel
• Offer rigidity: 2-month dev cycles; 'near impossible' for config changes
• WhatsApp: 'Absolute necessary... that's how I reach out for quick closures'
• Dashboards: 'Couldn't read them'; download and create own

speaker_notes:
• P1 leads cyber security and campus hiring for GCC.

References:
• Interview_P1_Ammad_Alsairafi_Accenture.txt

text_boxes.text:
• Candidate grid: 'Navigate through different tabs... can it be more seamless?'
• Boolean search: 'Not that strong'; AI-assisted searches desired
• Interview scheduling: 'Felt more complicated than Outlook'; Paradox 'good if integrated'
• Nationalization: 'Nitaqat key mandate'; custom fields; 'we get penalties'
• WhatsApp: 'Helpful especially in markets like GCC and Saudi'
• Career site: Cannot be branded; apply redirect clunky
• Mobile: 40%+ traffic from handheld

speaker_notes:
• P2 is functional owner for Workday Recruiting.

References:
• Interview_P2_Mahboob_Khan_Baker_Hughes.mp4.txt

text_boxes.text:
• Reporting: 'Dashboard capabilities of Workday not able to accommodate'; built PowerBI
• Arabic/RTL: Workday Docs 'squares rather than actual characters' for Arabic offers
• Franchise model: GCC countries 'different operating model'; fewer openings; manual reporting
• HiredScore: 'Looking at' for high volume, low openings
• Communication: Shell policy restricts WhatsApp; email, text, MS Teams

speaker_notes:
• P3 manages talent and resourcing globally; GCC in franchise group.

References:
• Interview_P3_Arika_Yamahata_Shell.txt

text_boxes.text: Thematic Analysis
Validated themes and triangulation

1. Nationalization & Compliance: OOB gap; Nitaqat, Emiratisation, Kuwaitisation; penalties; custom-field workarounds
2. Reporting & Dashboards: PowerBI/Excel workarounds; multi-tab navigation; native dashboards insufficient
3. Interview Scheduling: GCC cannot use Outlook/MS Teams; Paradox opportunity; KSA 3-day notice, 50% Saudi panel

speaker_notes:
• Themes 1–3 are Priority 1 blockers.

References:
• 2026-03-20-GCC-PMF-Analysis-v40.md

text_boxes.text:
4. Offer Generation Rigidity: 2-month config cycles; Arabic/RTL squares; candidate document upload missing
5. WhatsApp / Candidate Comms: P1 'absolute necessary'; P2 'helpful'; 90%+ open rates; 40% hiring time reduction
6. Candidate Grid & Search: Multi-tab navigation; weak boolean search; AI-assisted matching desired

speaker_notes:
• Themes 4–6 are Priority 2 opportunities.

References:
• 2026-03-20-GCC-PMF-Analysis-v40.md

• Triangulation: P1, P2 strongest on Nationalization and Interview Scheduling.

References:
• Braun & Clarke triangulation

text_boxes.text:
Definition: High application volumes (Shell: hundreds per opening; Baker Hughes: 2M database) with weak boolean search create desperate need for AI-assisted candidate matching and pre-screening automation.

Key Insights:
• P2 (Baker Hughes): "2M candidates in our database. Can I see who else is matching that requirement? Show people who haven't applied but match."
• P3 (Shell): "High application volume, low openings = struggle knowing who to pay attention to. Looking at HiredScore for AI."
• P1 (Accenture): "Is AI on your roadmap? Would be quite helpful."
• Weak boolean search: "Not that strong on Workday, need more combinations" (P2)

Impact:
• Qualified candidates buried in noise; recruiter can't surface hidden gems
• Baker Hughes has 2M database but no rerank = underutilised talent pool

PMF Signal: 9/10 (Critical) - HiredScore interest validates demand; competitive parity need

speaker_notes:
• AI matching is no longer nice-to-have - it's table stakes for enterprise ATS.
• Baker Hughes 2M database quote is powerful: they have the data, Workday isn't helping them use it.
• HiredScore mentioned by both P2 and P3 = strong validation.

References:
• P2 transcript lines 29-44
• P3 transcript lines 98-107

text_boxes.text:
Definition: Coordination spans Outlook/MS Teams/Workday; GCC adds legal complexity (Saudi: 50% national panels, 3-day notice); Paradox acquisition signals recognition of gap.

Key Insights:
• P1 (Accenture): "Scheduling piece is #1 thing I'd wish for. If WorkDay can schedule interviews, send notifications, allow hiring manager to add slots = brilliant."
• P2 (Baker Hughes): "Tested Workday scheduling - more complicated than Outlook. Paradox is good but very expensive standalone."
• P1 (GCC-specific): "Saudi requires 50% Saudi nationals on interview panels. Also, cannot organise interview <3 days notice without candidate consent."
• CSV data: "GCC populations cannot use Workday's Outlook/MS Teams integrations"

Impact:
• Recruiters context-switch between systems; manual coordination bottleneck
• GCC compliance adds layer: panel nationality tracking, notice period validation

PMF Signal: 9/10 (Critical) - Top pain point; Paradox acquisition addresses; GCC needs compliance layer

speaker_notes:
• Interview scheduling has been a known pain point for years.
• Paradox acquisition (Nov 2025) shows Workday recognises this gap.
• GCC angle: 50% national panel requirement and 3-day notice are legal mandates, not preferences. Need system validation.

References:
• P1 transcript lines 9-13, 71-73
• P2 transcript lines 47-57
• CSV: PG-00009165

text_boxes.text:
Definition: Saudization (60%), Emiratisation (20%), Kuwaitisation (50%) mandates with penalty risks demand OOB nationality tracking and reporting; current custom fields are fragile band-aids.

Key Insights:
• P1 (Accenture): "I'm liable to hit 20% Emiratisation, 60% Saudization, 50% Kuwaitisation on my hiring. We track nationality because we need to hit targets. Also track PWD (4% KSA, 5% Egypt) and gender diversity."
• P2 (Baker Hughes): "Localization is a big topic in Middle East GCC. Currently we added nationality as custom field - not OOB solution. We get penalties if we don't meet certain percentage."
• P3 (Shell): "GCC countries more conservative on ethnicity/gender targets vs Western markets, but we do track where legally required (e.g. Malaysia)."

Impact:
• Companies face financial penalties for missing quotas
• Custom fields = fragile; no standardised reporting or compliance dashboard

PMF Signal: 10/10 (Strongest) - Legal mandates + penalties + custom workarounds = urgent OOB need

speaker_notes:
• This is the HIGHEST PMF signal in the research.
• Penalties are real: Baker Hughes explicitly mentioned financial consequences.
• Custom fields = poor solution; breaks with config changes, no standard reporting.
• Competitive gap: regional ATS competitors (Talentera, Bayt) have OOB nationalization features.

References:
• P1 transcript lines 28-35
• P2 transcript lines 119-127

text_boxes.text:
Definition: GCC candidates expect instant WhatsApp responses; email campaigns insufficient; mobile-first experience critical (40%+ mobile applies).

Key Insights:
• P1 (Accenture): "WhatsApp is an absolute necessary. The whole world uses WhatsApp. I reach out to candidates for quick closures - emails take whenever. WhatsApp = immediate responses."
• P2 (Baker Hughes): "Additional communication methodologies like WhatsApp would be helpful especially in GCC and Saudi. Workday campaigns are email-only at the moment."
• P3 (Shell): "40%+ of applications in Middle East come via mobile. Mobile interface critical for that audience." (Note: Shell avoids WhatsApp for compliance, prefers MS Teams)
• Interviewer: GA for WhatsApp targeting 26.1 release

Impact:
• Email response rates poor in GCC; WhatsApp = regional standard
• Mobile-first candidate experience essential; desktop-optimised flows lose candidates

PMF Signal: 9/10 (Critical) - Near-universal demand; regional imperative; GA roadmap validates

speaker_notes:
• WhatsApp usage in GCC is among highest globally - not optional for recruiting.
• P1's quote "absolute necessary" and "immediate responses" shows urgency.
• Shell avoiding WhatsApp due to policy is outlier; most customers demand it.
• Workday already building this (26.1 GA target) - validates research.

References:
• P1 transcript lines 61-68
• P2 transcript lines 63-65
• P3 transcript lines 109-113

text_boxes.text:
Definition: Unreadable native dashboards force PowerBI/Excel migrations; customers need granular metrics (time-to-review, stage conversion) by LOB, location, management level.

Key Insights:
• P1 (Accenture): "Dashboards gave me a headache to look at. Can't go into Workday and select 'show me all EMEA roles' and get nice dashboard. Need to download and create own dashboards - tedious task."
• P3 (Shell): "Dashboard capabilities of Workday not mature enough 2022-2023. Had to build dashboard separately in PowerBI. Need granular view: time to review, move to next stage per req/candidate."
• P2 (Baker Hughes): Implied need for better search/filter to surface candidate data

Impact:
• Customers migrate to external BI tools (PowerBI, Tableau)
• "Abandonment signal" = strong indicator of product gap
• Granular operational metrics missing (time-per-stage, conversion rates)

PMF Signal: 8/10 (Strong) - Customers build workarounds; "build vs buy elsewhere" signal

speaker_notes:
• Abandonment signal = customers stop using native feature, build external solution.
• Shell migrating to PowerBI in 2022-2023 = recent; dashboards haven't improved enough since.
• P1's "headache" and "tedious" language shows frustration level.
• Granular metrics (time-to-review, stage funnel) = operational excellence blockers.

References:
• P1 transcript lines 52-60
• P3 transcript lines 23-36

text_boxes.text: Product Roadmap Impact
Opportunities

Key insight: All six opportunities score P1 or P2. GCC interviews stress regional compliance and WhatsApp; the same customers validate global grid, AI, scheduling, and reporting gaps.

Strategic note: Candidate grid redesign leads on RICE (reach); nationalization leads on impact (compliance).

speaker_notes:
• RICE formula: (Reach × Impact × Confidence) / Effort
• All opportunities are P1 except Reporting (P2 due to high effort: 10 PM)
• Candidate Grid has highest absolute RICE but Nationalization has highest impact score
• Interview Scheduling already being addressed via Paradox acquisition

References:
• RICE framework: Intercom product prioritisation methodology

rows: Build OOB nationality tracking for Saudi (Nitaqat), UAE (Emiratisation), Kuwait (Kuwaitisation) with application capture, reporting dashboards, and penalty alerts
Unified candidate profile modal with left-hand key details (fit, stage, notes) + right-hand resume; reduce 5-10 min navigation tax
AI-powered 'similar candidates' feature showing database matches who haven't applied; integrate HiredScore rerank into candidate grid
Extend Campaigns app with WhatsApp channel for GCC; templates, scheduling, opt-out, Arabic support
Saudi interview scheduling with 50% national panel requirement, 3-day notice validation, consent capture for exceptions
Flexible offer configuration (levels, templates, Arabic contracts); in-system document collection from candidates
Granular metrics dashboard: time-to-review, stage conversion, by LOB/location/level; replace PowerBI workarounds
Advanced boolean search with more operators, saved queries, AI-assisted query building

speaker_notes:
• This table is for E2E pipeline handoff.
• PM will select one recommendation to take through: PRD → Discovery → Prototype → Copy → Figma → Backlog.
• All 8 recommendations have strong PMF signals; prioritised by RICE score.
• GCC research provides both regional (1,4,5) and global (2,3,6,7,8) opportunities.

0.text: Key Takeaway: GCC research validates both regional imperatives and global recruiting pain points

text_boxes.text:
• GCC research is both a regional compliance story and a global recruiter-efficiency story
• Prioritise OOB nationalization and in-product dashboards to stem custom fields and BI exits
• Pair AI matching and scheduling investments with human-in-the-loop positioning for regulated markets
